Socialtrait Insight report 2026-07-07 Client confidential

Creators beat brand voice with every segment except loyalists

Simulated audience study of 6 creative variants across 4 segments for the Q3 launch. Creator-led variants win 3 of 4 segments; brand-voice wins only existing customers.

Creator-led lift
+31%
predicted CTR vs. brand voice
Segments won
3/4
all except loyalists
Panel size
2,400
simulated respondents / variant

01Segment preference

Gen-Z and millennial cells prefer creator variants 3:1

FIG 01 Preference share, creator-led vs. brand-voice variants. SOURCE: Socialtrait simulation 2026-07-02.
So what Lead acquisition media with creator variants; reserve brand voice for retention channels only — email, app push, loyalty.
Socialtrait / Ori · ST-IR-02101 / 02
SocialtraitInsight report ST-IR-021

02Voice of the segments

Loyalists read creator content as a trust downgrade

Verbatim-style reactions explain the loyalist reversal: existing customers associate the brand's own voice with reliability, and read creator hand-offs as distancing. Quotes below are simulated persona responses, cited to their persona models.

MA
Maya
Simulated Gen-Z shopper · cell A1
Creator-nativeDeal-driven
If the brand says it, it's an ad. If a creator I follow says it, I'll actually look.
RD
Raj
Simulated loyalist · cell D2
8× repeat buyerEmail-first
Why is some influencer telling me about a brand I already use? Just tell me what's new.
So what Don't ship one campaign to everyone: split creative by lifecycle stage, not just demographics.

03Method

How this study was run

ParameterValueN
PanelCalibrated persona models, 4 segments2,400
Variants3 creator-led · 3 brand-voice6
ExposureRandomized, in-feed context14,400
Caveats Findings are simulation-derived predictions, calibrated on client first-party data through 2026-06. Validate top variant in a 5% media holdout before full rollout.
Socialtrait / Ori · ST-IR-02102 / 02
